methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $8M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
PierSight is a Seed-stage Space & Aerospace company that has raised $8M to date. The broader sector includes 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers ranging from SpaceX to Firefly Aerospace, though none provide sufficient narrow-stage overlap for direct peer median comparison.

Stage-progression analysis is the only applicable triangulation method given data constraints. Using total raised ($8M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier yields an implied valuation of $20M. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied without target revenue disclosure. Peer-median valuation failed due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the private set—while 23 total Space & Aerospace peers exist, filtering for comparable scale and stage maturity left fewer than three datapoints.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $17M–$23M, with a median of $20M. This range reflects modest expansion around the stage-progression output to account for inherent uncertainty in the 2.5x multiplier, which itself varies by market conditions and investor appetite. Key uncertainties include: product-market fit validation, near-term revenue trajectory, and capital efficiency relative to sector norms. Without disclosed revenue or a richer private-peer set at matching stage and scale, the valuation remains anchored primarily to the mechanistic stage step-up rather than market comparables.
